title: Debugging FitDiagnostics / Closed range without finding crossing

posts.name: Johan Wilfried Wulff
posts.text:
Dear Combine Experts,
I’m trying to run FitDiagnostics on a single datacard and it is failing with the error message [ERROR] Closed range without finding crossing!. So in order to estimate where the crossing could be I ran a scan with MultiDimFit and it gives the following result:
image1132×921 76.5 KB
So when I run the fit within a range of r=-3,3, I get the following output:
```
Running RobustFit for POI r. Configured with strategy 0
Searching for crossing at nll = 449.879, in the interval -0.740846 < r < 3
r lvl-here lvl-there 	stepping
-0.366761    +0.26452  +0.49447    0.374085
0.007323    -0.42617  +0.26452    0.374085
-0.254536    +0.10341  -0.42617    0.112225
-0.142310    -0.09826  +0.10341    0.112225
-0.220868    +0.04722  -0.09826    0.033668
-0.187201    -0.01266  +0.04722    0.033668
-0.220710    +0.04688  -0.01266    0.000158
-0.220631    +0.04672  +0.04688    0.000079
-0.220592    +0.04663  +0.04672    0.000039
[ERROR] Closed range without finding crossing!
Searching for crossing at nll = 449.879, in the interval -0.740846 < r < -3
r lvl-here lvl-there 	stepping
(…)
[WARNING]: Unable to determine uncertainties on all fit parameters in s+b fit. Have a look at https://cms-analysis.github.io/HiggsAnalysis-CombinedLimit/part3/nonstandard/#fit-parameter-uncertainties for more information.
Fit S+B, status = 0, numBadNLL = 0, covariance quality = 2
— FitDiagnostics —
Best fit r: -0.740846  -0.550351/+3.74085  (68% CL)
```
`combine --method FitDiagnostics workspace.root --verbose 1 --mass 125.0 --robustFit 1 --redefineSignalPOIs r --setParameterRanges r=-3.0,3.0 --setParameters r=1.0  --stepSize 0.1 --maxFailedSteps 10 --skipBOnlyFit  --cminDefaultMinimizerType Minuit2 --cminDefaultMinimizerStrategy 0 --cminDefaultMinimizerTolerance 0.1 --cminFallbackAlgo Minuit2,0:0.2 --cminFallbackAlgo Minuit2,0:0.4 --X-rtd REMOVE_CONSTANT_ZERO_POINT=1`
It looks to me that combine is closing the range at a value of r which is practically almost exactly at the -68% crossing in the above plot. My question is: how can i debug this fit? I already increased the number of steps from 5 to 10 (before it was saying maximum number of failed steps reached
Thanks for any suggestions
posts.cooked_html:
Dear Combine Experts,

I’m trying to run FitDiagnostics on a single datacard and it is failing with the error message [ERROR] Closed range without finding crossing!. So in order to estimate where the crossing could be I ran a scan with MultiDimFit and it gives the following result:

image1132×921 76.5 KB

So when I run the fit within a range of r=-3,3, I get the following output:

```
Running RobustFit for POI r. Configured with strategy 0
Searching for crossing at nll = 449.879, in the interval -0.740846 < r < 3
r lvl-here lvl-there stepping
-0.366761 +0.26452 +0.49447 0.374085
0.007323 -0.42617 +0.26452 0.374085
-0.254536 +0.10341 -0.42617 0.112225
-0.142310 -0.09826 +0.10341 0.112225
-0.220868 +0.04722 -0.09826 0.033668
-0.187201 -0.01266 +0.04722 0.033668
-0.220710 +0.04688 -0.01266 0.000158
-0.220631 +0.04672 +0.04688 0.000079
-0.220592 +0.04663 +0.04672 0.000039
[ERROR] Closed range without finding crossing!
Searching for crossing at nll = 449.879, in the interval -0.740846 < r < -3
r lvl-here lvl-there stepping

(…)

[WARNING]: Unable to determine uncertainties on all fit parameters in s+b fit. Have a look at https://cms-analysis.github.io/HiggsAnalysis-CombinedLimit/part3/nonstandard/#fit-parameter-uncertainties for more information.
Fit S+B, status = 0, numBadNLL = 0, covariance quality = 2

— FitDiagnostics —
Best fit r: -0.740846 -0.550351/+3.74085 (68% CL)
```

`combine --method FitDiagnostics workspace.root --verbose 1 --mass 125.0 --robustFit 1 --redefineSignalPOIs r --setParameterRanges r=-3.0,3.0 --setParameters r=1.0 --stepSize 0.1 --maxFailedSteps 10 --skipBOnlyFit --cminDefaultMinimizerType Minuit2 --cminDefaultMinimizerStrategy 0 --cminDefaultMinimizerTolerance 0.1 --cminFallbackAlgo Minuit2,0:0.2 --cminFallbackAlgo Minuit2,0:0.4 --X-rtd REMOVE_CONSTANT_ZERO_POINT=1`

It looks to me that combine is closing the range at a value of r which is practically almost exactly at the -68% crossing in the above plot. My question is: how can i debug this fit? I already increased the number of steps from 5 to 10 (before it was saying maximum number of failed steps reached

Thanks for any suggestions

Can you please share your datacard so we can try to reproduce the error?
Thank you,
Giacomo

datacard_cat_2018_tautau_resolved2b_first_os_iso_spin_2_mass_300.txt (47.6 KB)

posts.name: Giacomo Ortona
posts.text:
Hi Johan,
As far as I can tell, everything seems to be correct in the model.
Reducing the step size seems to be solving your issue (I used —stepSize 0.05), although I am not really sure why the algorithm can’t find the crossing with the default value.
Best,
Giacomo

posts.name: Johan Wilfried Wulff
posts.text:
Hello Giacomo,
could you perhaps share the command you’re executing? I do not see the fit converging with a step size of 0.05.
Best,
posts.cooked_html:
Hello Giacomo,

could you perhaps share the command you’re executing? I do not see the fit converging with a step size of 0.05.

Best,

posts.name: Giacomo Ortona
posts.text:
Hi Johan,
Sure, here they are:
combine -M FitDiagnostics datacard_cat_2018_tautau_resolved2b_first_os_iso_spin_2_mass_300.txt --mass 125.0 --robustFit 1 --setParameterRanges r=-3.0,3.0 --stepSize 0.05 --maxFailedSteps 10 --skipBOnlyFit --cminDefaultMinimizerType Minuit2 --cminDefaultMinimizerStrategy 0 --cminDefaultMinimizerTolerance 0.1 --cminFallbackAlgo Minuit2,0:0.2 --cminFallbackAlgo Minuit2,0:0.4
<<< Combine >>>
<<< v10.5.1 >>>
>>> Random number generator seed is 123456
